entry.title: New Popeyes x Hot Ones Menu Arrives
entry.summary:
Popeyes partners with popular YouTube series Hot Ones to debut the new Popeyes x Hot Ones menu, featuring Hot Ones-inspired dishes with varying degrees of heat.
Available for a limited time at participating locations nationwide, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu includes:
Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers (Mild) - Pickled garlic sriracha-marinated Chicken Dippers, served with a Buttermilk Ranch dip cup and a Hot Ones Sriracha sachet. Smokin' Rojo Sandwich (Medium)- A crispy-fried chicken filet topped with Hot Ones' Los Calientes Rojo spread and pickles, on a toasted brioche bun. Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (Hot) - Your choice of bone-in or boneless wings dusted with a Ghost Pepper dry rub and served with a Hot Ones Last Dab Ranch dip cup. The Last Dab (Extremely Hot) - A sachet of Hot Ones' spiciest, signature hot sauce. It's available a la carte for $1.
For those who want to do their own Hot Ones-type challenge, Popeyes has put together the Popeyes x Hot Ones bundle. It includes the Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers, a Sriracha sachet, a Smokin' Rojo Sandwich, and Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (bone-in or boneless) with Last Dab Ranch for $16.99. As part of the collaboration, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu will be featured on an episode of the show, making Popeyes the first wing brand to be spotlighted on the show. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!

The Electric Punch Energy Refresca features a tropical punch-flavored, fizzy drink with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana for energy. It contains 160mg of caffeine. The Berry Burst Energy Refresca is a berry-flavored, carbonated drink infused with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana. It contains 160mg of caffeine. Both drinks are available for a suggested price of $4.99. Taco Bell currently serves the Tropical Punch Rockstar Energy Refresca and the Pineapple Lime Rockstar Energy Refresca nationwide for the same suggested price. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco John's Introduces New Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps
entry.summary:
Taco John's serves up new Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps for $1.79 each. Each wrap features a familiar combination of a crispy-fried white-meat chicken tender, shredded lettuce, a shredded four-cheese blend, and ranch, rolled up in a warm flour tortilla.
Regular and spicy versions are available, with the only difference between the two being the addition of pickled jalapenos in the spicy variety. For a limited time, Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps are also available as part of a two-for-$3 mix-and-match deal. You can also get them as part of the $5 Jr. Crispy Chicken Wrap Meal, which includes two of the wraps and Jr. Potato Olés. In addition to the new snackable wraps, Taco John's has made several of its Meal Steals available for in-person ordering (the value combos were previously only available when ordering online). The following Meal Steals are now available to order in person as well as digitally:
$5 Meal Steal - A Crispy Beef Taco, a Bean Burrito, Jr. Potato Olés, and a small fountain drink. $7 Meal Steal - Two Crispy Beef Tacos, a Nacho Crunch Beef Burrito, Jr. Potato Olés, and a small fountain drink. $9 Meal Steal - A Super Crispy Beef Taco, a Chicken Stuffed Grilled Taco, Jr. Potato Olés, a Churro, and a small fountain drink.
The $7 Burrito Bundle and $7 Taco Takeover remain as online-only specials. Photo via Taco John's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Never Ending Pasta Bowl is Back at Olive Garden
entry.summary:
The all-you-can-eat Never Ending Pasta Bowl promotion is back at Olive Garden for a limited time through Sunday, November 16, 2025.
Starting at $13.99, you can choose from four pastas, six sauces, and three all-you-can-eat toppings (for an additional $4.99. The Never Ending Pasta Bowl selection includes:
Pastas - Angel Hair, Fettuccine, Rigatoni, and Spaghetti Sauces - Alfredo, Five Cheese Marinara, Meat Sauce, and Spicy Three-Meat Sauce. Toppings - Crispy Chicken Fritta, Meatballs, and Italian Sausage
The Spicy Three-Meat Sauce is new to the promotion. It's also fairly new to Olive Garden, as it was introduced earlier this year. It's a meat sauce flavored with cherry peppers, chili flakes, garlic, herbs, pepperoni, red bell pepper, and red wine. Your meal also includes unlimited soup or salad, plus unlimited breadsticks. Tips for making the most of the promotion include asking for smaller portions to sample the various offerings and asking them to prep your next bowl as you're working on your current one, as there can be a bit of a wait otherwise. Photo via Olive Garden. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Testing New Cantina Chicken Double Tostada
entry.summary:
Taco Bell is testing the new Cantina Chicken Double Tostada for a limited time at participating locations in the Austin, TX area.
Resembling Taco Bell's Mexican Pizza, the new test item features a layer of slow-roasted chicken and black beans sandwiched by two crunchy tostada shells, topped with Avocado Verde sauce, a melted three-cheese blend (pepper jack, mozzarella, and cheddar), reduced-fat sour cream, purple cabbage, and pico de gallo. As with other Cantina Chicken menu items, a packet of Avocado Verde Salsa is included on the side. The Cantina Chicken Double Tostada is priced at $6.99. It's also available in a combo that includes a Cantina Chicken Double Tostada, a Soft Taco, and an Aqua Refresca for $10.49. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Fresh Fit Menu Returns to Subway
entry.summary:
Subway brings back its better-for-you Fresh Fit menu nationwide on September 17, 2025 (some locations are already offering the menu).
Each sandwich on the Fresh Fit menu features 20g or more protein, a full serving of veggies, and less than 500 calories in a six-inch sub. They include: - Grilled Chicken & Avocado (35g protein) - Grilled chicken, smashed avocado, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, and smoky Baja Chipotle sauce on multigrain bread. - Ham & Turkey Stacker (20g protein) - Oven-roasted turkey and Black Forest ham, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, and yellow mustard on multigrain bread. - Seasoned Steak & Avocado (35g protein) - Seasoned steak, smashed avocado, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, and red onions on multigrain bread. - Turkey & Ranch Delite (26g protein) - Oven-roasted turkey, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, red onions, and Peppercorn Ranch on multigrain bread. According to Subway, the new menu "returns with more flavor and options to fuel today's active lifestyles." Photo via Subway. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Krispy Kreme Launches New Fall Fair Donuts
entry.summary:
Krispy Kreme turns to fairs for inspiration in launching its new Fall Fair Collection of seasonal donuts.
Available for a limited time at participating locations chain-wide, the collection includes four donuts: - Cotton Candy Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in blue icing and cotton candy-flavored sugar, then topped with a strawberry-flavored icing swirl and a dollop of cotton candy-flavored buttercreme. - Kreme Puff Donut - A ring donut topped with a swirl of Kreme, a Donut Dot (donut hole), and a dusting of sweet powdered coating. - Blue Ribbon Apple Pie Donut - A shell donut with apple cinnamon filling, dipped in caramel-flavored icing, sprinkled with cinnamon cookie crumbs, and decorated with a white icing cross-hatch. - Caramel Churro Donut - A ring donut covered in cinnamon sugar and drizzled with caramel-flavored icing. Krispy Kreme's Fall Fair Collection can be purchased individually and by the dozen. Photo via Krispy Kreme. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Panera Brings Back Ciabatta Dippers
entry.summary:
Ciabatta Dippers are back on the menu at Panera for a limited time nationwide, and are available in two varieties: the returning French Dipper and the new Chicken Pesto Dipper.
The melty sandwiches come with a portion of soup for dipping. The French Dipper features marinated steak, provolone, asiago, and garlic aioli on ciabatta, served with Bistro French Onion Soup. The new Chicken Pesto Dipper slots in for the previous Spicy Chicken Dipper and includes grilled chicken, provolone, parmesan, and basil pesto on ciabatta, paired with Creamy Tomato Soup. Prices vary, but the French Dipper costs $13.69 near me, while the Chicken Pesto Dipper is $11.99. Photo via Panera. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Crumbl Bakes Cinnamon Roll Cheesecake and More Through September 20, 2025
entry.summary:
Crumbl bakes a Cinnamon Roll Cheesecake and more for this week's rotation of baked desserts. Here's what the lineup at Crumbl looks like from September 15 through September 20, 2025:
- Cinnamon Roll Cheesecake -Cinnamon cheesecake on a cinnamon-spiced graham cracker crust, swirled with a buttery cinnamon topping, and topped with piping of cream cheese glaze and a rossette of whipped cream. - Raspberry Cupcake Cookie - A warm, vanilla cupcake cookie topped with raspberry cream cheese frosting and white sprinkles. - S'mores Skillet Cookie featuring Hershey's Chocolate - A graham cookie baked with pieces of Hershey's Chocolate, topped with marshmallow mousse, a drizzle of melted chocolate, and graham cracker streusel. - Chocolate Milkshake Cookie - A chilled chocolate cookie rolled in chocolate sprinkles, topped with a chocolate mousse and a whipped cream rosette. - Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt. - Secret Menu - There will be two "secret menu" cookies for this week, which are chosen by each location. You can check what's available near you through the Crumbl website or mobile app. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Wendy's Switching to Shredded Lettuce in Burgers
entry.summary:
Wendy's is in the process of quietly switching from whole-leafed romaine lettuce to shredded iceberg lettuce for its burgers and chicken sandwiches. The switch began this summer at some locations and recently appeared here in Los Angeles.
The move was reportedly made to improve lettuce consistency and maintain a reliable supply. Where the romaine lettuce was hand-leafed at each location, the shredded iceberg lettuce arrives already prepared in bags, so there's a convenience factor as well. The lettuce change does not affect the chain's salads, which, depending on the salad, are made with romaine and/or spring mix. Personal preferences between shredded lettuce and leafed lettuce on a burger/sandwich can vary. Generally, leaf lettuce tends to wilt faster as there's typically more contact between the burger patty and the lettuce, while shredded lettuce occupies more volume, which provides more of a buffer against heat. However, shredded lettuce is also a lot messier as it falls out of your burger/sandwich easily. It's also cheaper. Additionally, for anyone who was getting lettuce-wrapped burgers, that's no longer an option at locations that have made the switch. Wendy's has not yet updated its website to reflect the change as of this writing (pictured). I recently got a burger from my local Wendy's and definitely noticed the shredded lettuce, but didn't note any mention of it during my visit. Photo via Wendy's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Chicken Finger Tacos Return to Zaxbys with a New Flavor
entry.summary:
Chicken Finger Tacos are back at Zaxbys and have brought along a new flavor: Buffalo Blue.
The new Buffalo Blue Taco features a hand-breaded chicken finger, pico de gallo, blue cheese crumbles, shredded iceberg lettuce, and a drizzle of Buffalo Garlic Blaze sauce and Ranch, served folded in a warm flour tortilla. The new taco is joined by the returning Chipotle Ranch with Pico Taco and Avocado Ranch BLT Taco. The Chipotle Ranch with Pico Taco includes a hand-breaded chicken finger, shredded iceberg lettuce, cheddar jack cheese, Pico de Gallo, and mildly spicy Chipotle Ranch sauce, in a warm flour tortilla. The Avocado Ranch BLT Taco features a hand-breaded chicken finger, sliced Roma tomatoes, hardwood-smoked bacon, shredded iceberg lettuce, and a drizzle of Avocado Ranch, served folded in a warmed flour tortilla. Chicken Finger Tacos are available for a limited time now through November 2, 2025. They're available a la carte or as part of a combo meal. Prices vary by location, but at a location in Atlanta, a single taco costs $3.99, while three tacos and a drink cost $11.29. Photo via Zaxbys. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Honey Roasted Peanut M&M's Arrive in Stores
entry.summary:
M&M's adds a sweet and salty twist on its classic Peanut variety with the launch of new Honey Roasted Peanut M&M's.
A permanent addition to the brand's candy lineup, the new variety features the same peanut covered in milk chocolate and a candy shell, only with an added sweet and salty, honey-roasted flavor. The new flavor comes in several sizes, including Single Size (1.63 oz), Share Size (2.83 oz), and Sharing Size stand-up pouches (8.6 oz). Honey Roasted M&M's will be available at retailers nationwide and on the M&M's website starting this month. Photo via M&M's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: White Castle Launches New BBQ Pulled Pork Slider and More
entry.summary:
White Castle kicks off the fall with the launch of the new BBQ Pulled Pork Slider, Triple Cheese Tots, and Pumpkin Latte Cheesecake-on-a-stick.
The BBQ Pulled Pork Slider features slow-roasted pulled pork smoked with hickory wood, mixed with a sweet BBQ sauce, and served with two pickle slices on a slider bun. You can get it a la carte or as part of a new $4.99 combo meal that includes two BBQ Pulled Pork Sliders, small fries, and a small drink. New Triple Cheese Tots are fried tater tots infused with bits of cheddar, Colby Jack, and mozzarella cheese. You get eight pieces per order. The new Pumpkin Latte Cheesecake-on-a-stick features a slice of pumpkin latte cheesecake on a crushed graham cracker and brown sugar crust served on a stick. All three menu items are available now for a limited time at participating locations. Photo via White Castle. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Chocolate Original Glazed Donuts are Back at Krispy Kreme Through September 14, 2025
entry.summary:
Krispy Kreme celebrates International Chocolate Day (Saturday, September 13) by bringing back its Chocolate Original Glazed Donuts now through Sunday, September 14, 2025.
The limited-time flavor features yeast ring donuts covered in a milk chocolate glaze. It is available by the dozen or individually. This is the second time that Krispy Kreme has brought back the Chocolate Original Glazed Donuts this year. As a promotion for the limited-time run, if you buy any dozen donuts at the regular price, you can get 50% off a dozen Chocolate Original Glazed Donuts. The deal is available when ordering in-person or online (using the code "CHOCOLATE"). Photo via Krispy Kreme. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Dairy Queen Welcomes Back Pumpkin Pie Blizzard for Fall 2025
entry.summary:
The Pumpkin Pie Blizzard has returned to the Dairy Queen menu for the fall, accompanied by two new treats: the Maple Cookie Shake and the Caramel Toffee Cookie Blizzard.
The Pumpkin Pie Blizzard features the chain's signature vanilla soft serve mixed with pumpkin pie pieces, crowned with whipped topping and a dusting of nutmeg. The new Maple Cookie Shake takes vanilla soft serve, blends it with a maple cookie crumble and milk, and finishes it off with whipped topping. The new Caramel Toffee Cookie Blizzard features vanilla soft serve blended with chewy cookie pieces, toffee, and caramel. The trio of seasonal items is available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide. Dairy Queen is also offering a limited-time deal through September 21, 2025, where DQ Rewards members can get a small Blizzard for 85 cents with a purchase of $1 or more through the DQ mobile app. Photo via Dairy Queen. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Shake Shack Debuts New French Onion Menu
entry.summary:
Heading into fall, Shake Shack launches a new, limited-time French onion soup-inspired menu headlined by the French Onion Soup Burger.
The menu is available at Shake Shacks nationwide on September 12, 2025, but is available now if you order through the Shake Shack website or mobile app. Shake Shack's French Onion Soup Burger features one or more quarter-pound Angus beef patties topped with Gruyere cheese, caramelized onions, crispy-fried sweet onions, and roasted garlic parmesan aioli on a toasted potato bun. The burger is available with up to three beef patties and starts at $11.79 with one patty here in Los Angeles. Other items on the French Onion Menu include: - French Onion 'Shroom Burger - A crisp-fried portobello mushroom filled with melted Muenster and cheddar cheeses, topped with caramelized onions and roasted garlic parmesan aioli on a toasted potato bun. It costs $11.79 near me. - French Onion Shack Stack - A quarter-pound Angus beef patty topped with Gruyere cheese, a 'Shroom Burger patty, caramelized onions, and roasted garlic parmesan aioli on a toasted potato bun. It costs $15.29 at my local Shake Shack. - Onion Rings - Crispy-fried, beer-battered sweet Spanish onion rings served with your choice of sauce. An order is $5.49 here. - Parmesan Garlic Fries - Crinkle-cut fries dusted with parmesan cheese and served with a side of roasted garlic parmesan aioli. It costs $5.99 near me. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Caramel Apple Cheesecake Cup Returns to Popeyes
entry.summary:
The Caramel Apple Cheesecake Cup is back at Popeyes for a limited time for fall 2025.
Formerly called the "Apple Caramel Cheesecake," the Caramel Apple Cheesecake Cup features cheesecake swirled with a sweet and tangy caramel apple filling on a buttery graham cracker crust. The limited-time dessert joins a selection that includes the Cinnamon Apple Pie and Strawberry Cream Cheese Pie. Prices vary, but a location here in Los Angeles sells the seasonal dessert for $3.79. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Insomnia Cookies Bakes New Chai Latte and Pumpkin Chocolate Chunk Cookies
entry.summary:
Insomnia Cookies introduces new Chai Latte and Pumpkin Chocolate Chunk Cookies as limited-time flavors for fall 2025.
The Chai Latte Cookie (pictured) features a cookie baked with warm chai spices and white chocolate chips, while the Pumpkin Chocolate Chunk Cookie is a spiced pumpkin cookie with chocolate chunks. Prices vary, but a location here in Los Angeles sells the fall cookies for $3.69 a piece (versus $3.29 for the other Classic cookies). The late-night cookie bakery chain will also be holding a PJ party at all its locations on September 16, 2025, from 8PM to close. Among the promotions for the festivities, Insomnia will be offering a free Classic cookie to its rewards members who show up in pajamas. Additionally, it will launch the Peanut Butter S'mores with Reese's Cookie that night. Photo via Insomnia Cookies. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Gotta Habit Meal Deals Return to Habit
entry.summary:
Gotta Habit Meal Deals are back for a limited time at Habit with a wider release following a limited test earlier this year. The promotion features three discounted combo meals: one for $6, one for $8, and one for $10.
Here's what you'll find in each meal: - $6 Gotta Habit Meal Deal - Half a BBQ Grilled Chicken Sandwich (a marinated grilled chicken breast filet, BBQ sauce, melted cheese, shredded lettuce, tomato, and mayo on grilled sourdough), a choice of side, and a regular fountain drink. - $8 Gotta Habit Meal Deal - Half a Chicken Club Sandwich (a marinated grilled chicken breast filet, bacon, avocado, lettuce, tomato, and mayo on grilled sourdough), a choice of side, and a regular fountain drink. - $10 Gotta Habit Meal Deal - Half a Santa Barbara Char Sandwich (a chargrilled beef patty, lettuce, tomato, caramelized onions, melted cheese, avocado, pickles, and mayo on grilled sourdough) served with five-piece chicken bites, a choice of side, and a large fountain drink. Gotta Habit Meal Deals are available now for a limited time at participating Habit Burger & Grill restaurants chain-wide. Photo via Habit Burger & Grill. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Welcomes New Grande Nachos
entry.summary:
Taco Bell adds new Grande Nachos to the menu for a limited time. The spiritual successor of the Grande Nacho Box features a double portion of meat with nacho cheese and various toppings served in a paper box.
You can get the nachos with seasoned beef or slow-roasted chicken. The Grande Nachos with Seasoned Beef includes a double portion of seasoned ground beef (compared to Nachos BellGrande), refried beans, warm nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar), pico de gallo, reduced-fat sour cream, and guacamole, served over a bed of tortilla chips for a suggested price of $5.99. The Grande Nachos with Slow-Roasted Chicken is the same, only with a double portion of slow-roasted chicken (versus the Cantina Chicken Soft Taco). It carries a suggested price of $6.99. The prices can vary. One Taco Bell near me sells the Seasoned Beef version for $7.89, while the Slow-Roasted Chicken version is $8.89, but another location offers them for the advertised prices of $5.99 and $6.99. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Cava Serves Up New Chicken Shawarma
entry.summary:
Mediterranean fast-casual restaurant chain Cava welcomes new Chicken Shawarma as a limited-time protein option.
Cava's version of Chicken Shawarma features chicken breasts marinated in a "signature " spice blend, stacked and roasted on a spit, and shaved thin. In addition to being available in a build-your-own pita or bowl, Chicken Shawarma is featured in two chef-curated menu items: - Garlicky Chicken Shawarma Bowl (pictured) - Chicken shawarma, red pepper hummus, Crazy Feta, corn, tomato, cucumber, pickled onions, fiery broccoli, arugula, saffron basmati rice, skhug, and garlic dressing. - Chicken Shawarma Pita - Chicken shawarma, hummus, tomato, onion, shredded romaine, salt-brined pickles, pickled onions, garlic dressing, and lemon herb tahini. Photo via Cava. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Baskin-Robbins Launches New Pumpkin Spice Cappy
entry.summary:
Baskin-Robbins readies for fall 2025 by launching the new Pumpkin Spice Cappy and bringing back Pumpkin Cheesecake ice cream for the season. "Cappy" is what the chain has renamed its Cappuccino Blasts.
The Pumpkin Spice Cappy features a blend of Arabica coffee, Pumpkin Cheesecake ice cream, and ice, topped with whipped cream and cinnamon. Baskin-Robbins' Pumpkin Cheesecake ice cream is the Flavor of the Month for September 2025 and features a combination of pumpkin and cheesecake ice creams with a cinnamon cream cheese swirl and gingersnap cookie pieces. In celebration of the fall, Baskin-Robbins is running a limited-time deal where you can get any Cappy drink (including the new Pumpkin Spice Cappy) at a discount. It's $5 for a small, $6 for a medium, and $7 for a large. The special is available at participating locations nationwide through October 31, 2025. Photo via Baskin-Robbins. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Carne Asada is Back at Chipotle
entry.summary:
Carne Asada is back on the menu at Chipotle for a limited time in the US and Canada.
First introduced in 2019 and returning for the fourth time, Chipotle's Carne Asada features marinated steak that's seasoned on the grill with a "signature" spice blend that includes cumin, coriander, garlic, and oregano. After cooking, it's sliced (rather than cubed like the chain's regular steak) and finished with fresh-squeezed lime and chopped cilantro. Prices vary, but at my local Chipotle in Los Angeles, a Carne Asada Burrito Bowl costs $13.10, while a Steak Burrito Bowl is $11.70 (a Chicken, Veggie, or Sofritas Bowl is cheapest at $9.95). In other news, Chipotle marks the start of football season by introducing its "Wear Your College Football Jersey" promotion on Monday, September 15, 2025, after 5PM (local time). During the promotion, you can get a buy-one-get-one-free deal on any entree if you show up wearing a college football jersey, Finally, as a launch promo for the return of Carne Asada, through Tuesday, September 30, 2025, you can redeem a $0 delivery fee offer on all Carne Asada orders placed through the Chipotle website or mobile app. Photo via Chipotle. Read more at Brand Eating!
